e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000 (cents). For RTP, the maximum amount
allowed by the network is $100,000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.originating_account_id
documentation: The ID of one of your organization's internal accounts.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.accounting_ledger_class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting ledger classes.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.charge_bearer
documentation:
The party that will pay the fees for the payment order.

Only applies to wire payment orders. Can be one of shared, sender, or receiver,
which correspond respectively with the SWIFT 71A values `SHA`, `OUR`, `BEN`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.currency
documentation: Defaults to the currency of the originating account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.effective_date
documentation:
Date transactions are to be posted to the participants' account.

Defaults to the current business day or the next business day if the current day
is a bank holiday or weekend. Format: yyyy-mm-dd.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.expires_at
documentation: RFP payments require an expires_at. This value must be past the effective_date.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.fallback_type
documentation:
A payment type to fallback to if the original type is not valid for the
receiving account. Currently, this only supports falling back from RTP to ACH
(type=rtp and fallback_type=ach)

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.foreign_exchange_contract
documentation:
If present, indicates a specific foreign exchange contract number that has been
generated by your financial institution.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.foreign_exchange_indicator
documentation:
Indicates the type of FX transfer to initiate, can be either
`variable_to_fixed`, `fixed_to_variable`, or `null` if the payment order
currency matches the originating account currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ledger_transaction
documentation:
Specifies a ledger transaction object that will be created with the payment
order. If the ledger transaction cannot be created, then the payment order
creation will fail. The resulting ledger transaction will mirror the status of
the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ledger_transaction_id
documentation:
Either ledger_transaction or ledger_transaction_id can be provided.

Only a pending ledger transaction can be attached upon payment order creation.
Once the payment order is created, the status of the ledger transaction tracks
the payment order automatically.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.line_items
documentation: An array of line items that must sum up to the amount of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.nsf_protected
documentation:
A boolean to determine if NSF Protection is enabled for this payment order.

Note that this setting must also be turned on in your organization settings
page.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.originating_party_name
documentation:
If present, this will replace your default company name on receiver's bank
statement. This field can only be used for ACH payments currently. For ACH, only
the first 16 characters of this string will be used. Any additional characters
will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.priority
documentation:
Either `normal` or `high`.

For ACH and EFT payments, `high` represents a same-day ACH or EFT transfer,
respectively. For check payments, `high` can mean an overnight check rather than
standard mail.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.process_after
documentation:
If present, Modern Treasury will not process the payment until after this time.

If `process_after` is past the cutoff for `effective_date`, `process_after` will
take precedence and `effective_date` will automatically update to reflect the
earliest possible sending date after `process_after`. Format is ISO8601
timestamp.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.purpose
documentation:
For `wire`, this is usually the purpose which is transmitted via the
"InstrForDbtrAgt" field in the ISO20022 file. For `eft`, this field is the 3
digit CPA Code that will be attached to the payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.receiving_account
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.receiving_account_id
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.send_remittance_advice
documentation:
Send an email to the counterparty when the payment order is sent to the bank.

If `null`, `send_remittance_advice` on the Counterparty is used.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.statement_descriptor
documentation:
An optional descriptor which will appear in the receiver's statement.

For `check` payments this field will be used as the memo line. For `ach` the
maximum length is 10 characters. Note that for ACH payments, the name on your
bank account will be included automatically by the bank, so you can use the
characters for other useful information. For `eft` the maximum length is 15
characters.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.subtype
documentation:
An additional layer of classification for the type of payment order you are
doing. This field is only used for `ach` payment orders currently. For `ach`
payment orders, the `subtype` represents the SEC code. We currently support
`CCD`, `PPD`, `IAT`, `CTX`, `WEB`, `CIE`, and `TEL`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.transaction_monitoring_enabled
documentation:
A flag that determines whether a payment order should go through transaction
monitoring.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ultimate_originating_party_identifier
documentation: Identifier of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ultimate_originating_party_name
documentation: Name of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ultimate_receiving_party_identifier
documentation: Identifier of the ultimate funds recipient.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderAsyncCreateRequest.ultimate_receiving_party_name
documentation: Name of the ultimate funds recipient.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.ledger_account_id
documentation: The ledger account that this ledger entry is associated with.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.available_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s available balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.lock_version
documentation:
Lock version of the ledger account.

This can be passed when creating a ledger transaction to only succeed if no
ledger transactions have posted since the given version. See our post about
Designing the Ledgers API with Optimistic Locking for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.pending_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s pending balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.posted_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s posted balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.show_resulting_ledger_account_balances
documentation:
If true, response will include the balance of the associated ledger account for
the entry.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.ledger_entries
documentation: An array of ledger entry objects.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.effective_at
documentation:
The timestamp (ISO8601 format) at which the ledger transaction happened for
reporting purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.effective_date
documentation:
The date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which the ledger transaction happened for reporting
purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.external_id
documentation:
A unique string to represent the ledger transaction.

Only one pending or posted ledger transaction may have this ID in the ledger.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.ledgerable_id
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the id will be populated here, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.ledgerable_type
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the type will be populated here, otherwise null. This can be one of
payment_order, incoming_payment_detail, expected_payment, return, paper_item, or
reversal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLedgerTransaction.status
documentation: To post a ledger transaction at creation, use `posted`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLineItem.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLineItem.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLineItem.description
documentation: A free-form description of the line item.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequestLineItem.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.amount_lower_bound
documentation:
The lowest amount this expected payment may be equal to.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented
as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.amount_upper_bound
documentation:
The highest amount this expected payment may be equal to.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented
as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.counterparty_id
documentation: The ID of the counterparty you expect for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.currency
documentation: Must conform to ISO 4217. Defaults to the currency of the internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.date_lower_bound
documentation: The earliest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.date_upper_bound
documentation: The latest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.direction
documentation:
One of credit or debit.

When you are receiving money, use credit. When you are being charged, use debit.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the Internal Account for the expected payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.ledger_transaction
documentation:
Specifies a ledger transaction object that will be created with the expected
payment. If the ledger transaction cannot be created, then the expected payment
creation will fail. The resulting ledger transaction will mirror the status of
the expected payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.ledger_transaction_id
documentation:
Either ledger_transaction or ledger_transaction_id can be provided.

Only a pending ledger transaction can be attached upon expected payment
creation. Once the expected payment is created, the status of the ledger
transaction tracks the expected payment automatically.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.reconciliation_filters
documentation: The reconciliation filters you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.reconciliation_groups
documentation: The reconciliation groups you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.reconciliation_rule_variables
documentation: An array of reconciliation rule variables for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.statement_descriptor
documentation:
The statement description you expect to see on the transaction.

For ACH payments, this will be the full line item passed from the bank. For wire
payments, this will be the OBI field on the wire. For check payments, this will
be the memo field.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentCreateRequest.type
documentation:
One of: ach, au_becs, bacs, book, check, eft, interac, provxchange, rtp, sen,
sepa, signet, wire.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.ledger_account_id
documentation: The ledger account that this ledger entry is associated with.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.available_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s available balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.lock_version
documentation:
Lock version of the ledger account.

This can be passed when creating a ledger transaction to only succeed if no
ledger transactions have posted since the given version. See our post about
Designing the Ledgers API with Optimistic Locking for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.pending_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s pending balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.posted_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s posted balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequestLedgerEntry.show_resulting_ledger_account_balances
documentation:
If true, response will include the balance of the associated ledger account for
the entry.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.ledger_entries
documentation: An array of ledger entry objects.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.effective_at
documentation:
The timestamp (ISO8601 format) at which the ledger transaction happened for
reporting purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.effective_date
documentation:
The date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which the ledger transaction happened for reporting
purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.external_id
documentation:
A unique string to represent the ledger transaction.

Only one pending or posted ledger transaction may have this ID in the ledger.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.ledgerable_id
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the id will be populated here, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.ledgerable_type
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the type will be populated here, otherwise null. This can be one of
payment_order, incoming_payment_detail, expected_payment, return, paper_item, or
reversal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionCreateRequest.status
documentation: To post a ledger transaction at creation, use `posted`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.as_of_date
documentation: The date on which the transaction occurred.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.direction
documentation: Either `credit` or `debit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the relevant Internal Account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.vendor_code
documentation:
When applicable, the bank-given code that determines the transaction's category.

For most banks this is the BAI2/BTRS transaction code.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.vendor_code_type
documentation:
The type of `vendor_code` being reported.

Can be one of `bai2`, `bankprov`, `bnk_dev`, `cleartouch`, `currencycloud`,
`cross_river`, `dc_bank`, `dwolla`, `evolve`, `goldman_sachs`, `iso20022`,
`jpmc`, `mx`, `signet`, `silvergate`, `swift`, `us_bank`, or others.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.posted
documentation: This field will be `true` if the transaction has posted to the account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.type
documentation:
The type of the transaction.

Examples could be `card, `ach`, `wire`, `check`, `rtp`, `book`, or `sen`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionCreateRequest.vendor_description
documentation:
The transaction detail text that often appears in on your bank statement and in
your banking portal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDAccounting.account_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDAccounting.class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of the class objects in your accounting system.

Class objects track segments of your business independent of client or project.
Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDLineItem.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDLineItem.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDLineItem.description
documentation: A free-form description of the line item.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDLineItem.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.ledger_id
documentation: The id of the ledger that this account belongs to.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.name
documentation: The name of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.normal_balance
documentation: The normal balance of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.description
documentation: The description of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.ledger_account_category_ids
documentation:
The array of ledger account category ids that this ledger account should be a
child of.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.ledgerable_id
documentation:
If the ledger account links to another object in Modern Treasury, the id will be
populated here, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.ledgerable_type
documentation:
If the ledger account links to another object in Modern Treasury, the type will
be populated here, otherwise null. The value is one of internal_account or
external_account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountLedgerAccount.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountPartyAddress.country
documentation: Country code conforms to [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2]

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountPartyAddress.locality
documentation: Locality or City.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountPartyAddress.postal_code
documentation: The postal code of the address.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccountPartyAddress.region
documentation: Region or State.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.account_type
documentation: Can be `checking`, `savings` or `other`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.ledger_account
documentation:
Specifies a ledger account object that will be created with the external
account.

The resulting ledger account is linked to the external account for
auto-ledgering Payment objects. See
https://docs.moderntreasury.com/docs/linking-to-other-modern-treasury-objects
for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.name
documentation:
A nickname for the external account.

This is only for internal usage and won't affect any payments

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.party_address
documentation: Required if receiving wire payments.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.party_name
documentation: If this value isn't provided, it will be inherited from the counterparty's name.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.party_type
documentation: Either `individual` or `business`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithIDReceivingAccount.plaid_processor_token
documentation:
If you've enabled the Modern Treasury + Plaid integration in your Plaid account,
you can pass the processor token in this field.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.accounting_ledger_class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting ledger classes.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000 (cents). For RTP, the maximum amount
allowed by the network is $100,000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.charge_bearer
documentation:
The party that will pay the fees for the payment order.

Only applies to wire payment orders. Can be one of shared, sender, or receiver,
which correspond respectively with the SWIFT 71A values `SHA`, `OUR`, `BEN`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.counterparty_id
documentation: Required when receiving_account_id is passed the ID of an external account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.currency
documentation: Defaults to the currency of the originating account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.effective_date
documentation:
Date transactions are to be posted to the participants' account.

Defaults to the current business day or the next business day if the current day
is a bank holiday or weekend. Format: yyyy-mm-dd.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.expires_at
documentation: RFP payments require an expires_at. This value must be past the effective_date.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.fallback_type
documentation:
A payment type to fallback to if the original type is not valid for the
receiving account. Currently, this only supports falling back from RTP to ACH
(type=rtp and fallback_type=ach)

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.foreign_exchange_contract
documentation:
If present, indicates a specific foreign exchange contract number that has been
generated by your financial institution.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.foreign_exchange_indicator
documentation:
Indicates the type of FX transfer to initiate, can be either
`variable_to_fixed`, `fixed_to_variable`, or `null` if the payment order
currency matches the originating account currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.line_items
documentation: An array of line items that must sum up to the amount of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.nsf_protected
documentation:
A boolean to determine if NSF Protection is enabled for this payment order.

Note that this setting must also be turned on in your organization settings
page.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.originating_account_id
documentation: The ID of one of your organization's internal accounts.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.originating_party_name
documentation:
If present, this will replace your default company name on receiver's bank
statement. This field can only be used for ACH payments currently. For ACH, only
the first 16 characters of this string will be used. Any additional characters
will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.priority
documentation:
Either `normal` or `high`.

For ACH and EFT payments, `high` represents a same-day ACH or EFT transfer,
respectively. For check payments, `high` can mean an overnight check rather than
standard mail.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.process_after
documentation:
If present, Modern Treasury will not process the payment until after this time.

If `process_after` is past the cutoff for `effective_date`, `process_after` will
take precedence and `effective_date` will automatically update to reflect the
earliest possible sending date after `process_after`. Format is ISO8601
timestamp.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.purpose
documentation:
For `wire`, this is usually the purpose which is transmitted via the
"InstrForDbtrAgt" field in the ISO20022 file. For `eft`, this field is the 3
digit CPA Code that will be attached to the payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.receiving_account
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.receiving_account_id
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.send_remittance_advice
documentation:
Send an email to the counterparty when the payment order is sent to the bank.

If `null`, `send_remittance_advice` on the Counterparty is used.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.statement_descriptor
documentation:
An optional descriptor which will appear in the receiver's statement.

For `check` payments this field will be used as the memo line. For `ach` the
maximum length is 10 characters. Note that for ACH payments, the name on your
bank account will be included automatically by the bank, so you can use the
characters for other useful information. For `eft` the maximum length is 15
characters.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.status
documentation:
To cancel a payment order, use `cancelled`.

To redraft a returned payment order, use `approved`. To undo approval on a
denied or approved payment order, use `needs_approval`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.subtype
documentation:
An additional layer of classification for the type of payment order you are
doing. This field is only used for `ach` payment orders currently. For `ach`
payment orders, the `subtype` represents the SEC code. We currently support
`CCD`, `PPD`, `IAT`, `CTX`, `WEB`, `CIE`, and `TEL`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.ultimate_originating_party_identifier
documentation:
This represents the identifier by which the person is known to the receiver when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 22 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.ultimate_originating_party_name
documentation:
This represents the name of the person that the payment is on behalf of when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 15 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.ultimate_receiving_party_identifier
documentation:
This represents the name of the merchant that the payment is being sent to when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 22 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourcePaymentOrderUpdateRequestWithID.ultimate_receiving_party_name
documentation:
This represents the identifier by which the merchant is known to the person
initiating an ACH payment with CIE subtype. Only the first 15 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.amount_lower_bound
documentation:
The lowest amount this expected payment may be equal to.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented
as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.amount_upper_bound
documentation:
The highest amount this expected payment may be equal to.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented
as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.counterparty_id
documentation: The ID of the counterparty you expect for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.currency
documentation: Must conform to ISO 4217. Defaults to the currency of the internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.date_lower_bound
documentation: The earliest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.date_upper_bound
documentation: The latest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.direction
documentation:
One of credit or debit.

When you are receiving money, use credit. When you are being charged, use debit.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the Internal Account for the expected payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.reconciliation_filters
documentation: The reconciliation filters you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.reconciliation_groups
documentation: The reconciliation groups you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.reconciliation_rule_variables
documentation: An array of reconciliation rule variables for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.statement_descriptor
documentation:
The statement description you expect to see on the transaction.

For ACH payments, this will be the full line item passed from the bank. For wire
payments, this will be the OBI field on the wire. For check payments, this will
be the memo field.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.status
documentation:
The Expected Payment's status can be updated from partially_reconciled to
reconciled.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceExpectedPaymentUpdateRequestWithID.type
documentation:
One of: ach, au_becs, bacs, book, check, eft, interac, provxchange, rtp, sen,
sepa, signet, wire.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.metadata
documentation:
Additional data in the form of key-value pairs.

Pairs can be removed by passing an empty string or `null` as the value.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.ledger_account_id
documentation: The ledger account that this ledger entry is associated with.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.available_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s available balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.lock_version
documentation:
Lock version of the ledger account.

This can be passed when creating a ledger transaction to only succeed if no
ledger transactions have posted since the given version. See our post about
Designing the Ledgers API with Optimistic Locking for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.pending_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s pending balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.posted_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s posted balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithIDLedgerEntry.show_resulting_ledger_account_balances
documentation:
If true, response will include the balance of the associated ledger account for
the entry.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.effective_at
documentation:
The timestamp (ISO8601 format) at which the ledger transaction happened for
reporting purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.ledger_entries
documentation: An array of ledger entry objects.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.ledgerable_id
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the id will be populated here, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.ledgerable_type
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the type will be populated here, otherwise null. This can be one of
payment_order, incoming_payment_detail, expected_payment, return, paper_item, or
reversal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.bulk_request_create_params.ResourceLedgerTransactionUpdateRequestWithID.status
documentation: To post a ledger transaction at creation, use `posted`.

modules.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.BankAddress.country
documentation: Country code conforms to [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2]

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.BankAddress.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.BankAddress.locality
documentation: Locality or City.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.BankAddress.postal_code
documentation: The postal code of the address.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.BankAddress.region
documentation: Region or State.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.RoutingDetail.bank_name
documentation: The name of the bank.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.RoutingDetail.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.RoutingDetail.payment_type
documentation:
If the routing detail is to be used for a specific payment type this field will
be populated, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.RoutingDetail.routing_number
documentation: The routing number of the bank.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.routing_detail.RoutingDetail.routing_number_type
documentation:
The type of routing number.

See https://docs.moderntreasury.com/platform/reference/routing-detail-object for
more details.

modules.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.as_of_date
documentation: The date on which the transaction occurred.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.direction
documentation: Either `credit` or `debit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the relevant Internal Account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.vendor_code
documentation:
When applicable, the bank-given code that determines the transaction's category.

For most banks this is the BAI2/BTRS transaction code.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.vendor_code_type
documentation:
The type of `vendor_code` being reported.

Can be one of `bai2`, `bankprov`, `bnk_dev`, `cleartouch`, `currencycloud`,
`cross_river`, `dc_bank`, `dwolla`, `evolve`, `goldman_sachs`, `iso20022`,
`jpmc`, `mx`, `signet`, `silvergate`, `swift`, `us_bank`, or others.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.posted
documentation: This field will be `true` if the transaction has posted to the account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.type
documentation:
The type of the transaction.

Examples could be `card, `ach`, `wire`, `check`, `rtp`, `book`, or `sen`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction_create_params.TransactionCreateParams.vendor_description
documentation:
The transaction detail text that often appears in on your bank statement and in
your banking portal.

modules.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.annual_income
documentation: The annual income of the individual.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.employer_country
documentation: The country in which the employer is located.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.employer_name
documentation: The name of the employer.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.employer_state
documentation: The state in which the employer is located.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.employment_status
documentation: The employment status of the individual.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.income_country
documentation: The country in which the individual's income is earned.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.wealth_and_employment_details.WealthAndEmploymentDetails.income_source

The posted balance is the sum of all posted entries on the account. The pending
balance is the sum of all pending and posted entries on the account. The
available balance is the posted incoming entries minus the sum of the pending
and posted outgoing amounts.

documentation:
The timestamp (ISO8601 format) at which the ledger transaction happened for
reporting purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.effective_date
documentation:
The date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which the ledger transaction happened for reporting
purposes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.external_id
documentation:
A unique string to represent the ledger transaction.

Only one pending or posted ledger transaction may have this ID in the ledger.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.ledgerable_id
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the id will be populated here, otherwise null.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.ledgerable_type
documentation:
If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
Treasury, the type will be populated here, otherwise null. This can be one of
payment_order, incoming_payment_detail, expected_payment, return, paper_item, or
reversal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerTransactionCreateParams.status
documentation: To post a ledger transaction at creation, use `posted`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.ledger_account_id
documentation: The ledger account that this ledger entry is associated with.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.available_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s available balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.lock_version
documentation:
Lock version of the ledger account.

This can be passed when creating a ledger transaction to only succeed if no
ledger transactions have posted since the given version. See our post about
Designing the Ledgers API with Optimistic Locking for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.pending_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s pending balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.posted_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s posted balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_transaction_create_params.LedgerEntry.show_resulting_ledger_account_balances
documentation:
If true, response will include the balance of the associated ledger account for
the entry.

When money leaves the virtual account, this ledger account will be credited.
Must be accompanied by a debit_ledger_account_id if present.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.VirtualAccountCreateParams.debit_ledger_account_id

When money enters the virtual account, this ledger account will be debited. Must
be accompanied by a credit_ledger_account_id if present.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.VirtualAccountCreateParams.description

The resulting ledger account is linked to the virtual account for auto-ledgering

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.LedgerAccount.currency_exponent

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.LedgerAccount.description

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.LedgerAccount.ledgerable_id

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.LedgerAccount.metadata

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.RoutingDetail.routing_number

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.virtual_account_create_params.RoutingDetail.payment_type

documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.
