documentation:
This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Please use LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry instead.

variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LineItems
documentation:
This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Please use LineItem instead.

variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.ReceivingAccountAccountDetails
documentation:
This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Please use ReceivingAccountAccountDetail instead.

variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.ReceivingAccountContactDetails
documentation:
This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Please use ReceivingAccountContactDetail instead.

variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.ReceivingAccountRoutingDetails
documentation:
This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

Please use ReceivingAccountRoutingDetail instead.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000 (cents). For RTP, the maximum amount
allowed by the network is $100,000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.originating_account_id
documentation: The ID of one of your organization's internal accounts.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.accounting_ledger_class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting ledger classes.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.charge_bearer
documentation:
The party that will pay the fees for the payment order.

Only applies to wire payment orders. Can be one of shared, sender, or receiver,
which correspond respectively with the SWIFT 71A values `SHA`, `OUR`, `BEN`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.currency
documentation: Defaults to the currency of the originating account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.effective_date
documentation:
Date transactions are to be posted to the participants' account.

Defaults to the current business day or the next business day if the current day
is a bank holiday or weekend. Format: yyyy-mm-dd.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.expires_at
documentation: RFP payments require an expires_at. This value must be past the effective_date.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.fallback_type
documentation:
A payment type to fallback to if the original type is not valid for the
receiving account. Currently, this only supports falling back from RTP to ACH
(type=rtp and fallback_type=ach)

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.foreign_exchange_contract
documentation:
If present, indicates a specific foreign exchange contract number that has been
generated by your financial institution.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.foreign_exchange_indicator
documentation:
Indicates the type of FX transfer to initiate, can be either
`variable_to_fixed`, `fixed_to_variable`, or `null` if the payment order
currency matches the originating account currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ledger_transaction
documentation:
Specifies a ledger transaction object that will be created with the payment
order. If the ledger transaction cannot be created, then the payment order
creation will fail. The resulting ledger transaction will mirror the status of
the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ledger_transaction_id
documentation:
Either ledger_transaction or ledger_transaction_id can be provided.

Only a pending ledger transaction can be attached upon payment order creation.
Once the payment order is created, the status of the ledger transaction tracks
the payment order automatically.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.line_items
documentation: An array of line items that must sum up to the amount of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.nsf_protected
documentation:
A boolean to determine if NSF Protection is enabled for this payment order.

Note that this setting must also be turned on in your organization settings
page.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.originating_party_name
documentation:
If present, this will replace your default company name on receiver's bank
statement. This field can only be used for ACH payments currently. For ACH, only
the first 16 characters of this string will be used. Any additional characters
will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.priority
documentation:
Either `normal` or `high`.

For ACH and EFT payments, `high` represents a same-day ACH or EFT transfer,
respectively. For check payments, `high` can mean an overnight check rather than
standard mail.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.process_after
documentation:
If present, Modern Treasury will not process the payment until after this time.

If `process_after` is past the cutoff for `effective_date`, `process_after` will
take precedence and `effective_date` will automatically update to reflect the
earliest possible sending date after `process_after`. Format is ISO8601
timestamp.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.purpose
documentation:
For `wire`, this is usually the purpose which is transmitted via the
"InstrForDbtrAgt" field in the ISO20022 file. For `eft`, this field is the 3
digit CPA Code that will be attached to the payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.receiving_account
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.receiving_account_id
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.send_remittance_advice
documentation:
Send an email to the counterparty when the payment order is sent to the bank.

If `null`, `send_remittance_advice` on the Counterparty is used.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.statement_descriptor
documentation:
An optional descriptor which will appear in the receiver's statement.

For `check` payments this field will be used as the memo line. For `ach` the
maximum length is 10 characters. Note that for ACH payments, the name on your
bank account will be included automatically by the bank, so you can use the
characters for other useful information. For `eft` the maximum length is 15
characters.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.subtype
documentation:
An additional layer of classification for the type of payment order you are
doing. This field is only used for `ach` payment orders currently. For `ach`
payment orders, the `subtype` represents the SEC code. We currently support
`CCD`, `PPD`, `IAT`, `CTX`, `WEB`, `CIE`, and `TEL`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.transaction_monitoring_enabled
documentation:
A flag that determines whether a payment order should go through transaction
monitoring.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ultimate_originating_party_identifier
documentation: Identifier of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ultimate_originating_party_name
documentation: Name of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ultimate_receiving_party_identifier
documentation: Identifier of the ultimate funds recipient.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.PaymentOrderCreateAsyncParams.ultimate_receiving_party_name
documentation: Name of the ultimate funds recipient.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.Accounting.account_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.Accounting.class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of the class objects in your accounting system.

Class objects track segments of your business independent of client or project.
Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.ledger_account_id
documentation: The ledger account that this ledger entry is associated with.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.available_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s available balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.lock_version
documentation:
Lock version of the ledger account.

This can be passed when creating a ledger transaction to only succeed if no
ledger transactions have posted since the given version. See our post about
Designing the Ledgers API with Optimistic Locking for more details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.pending_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s pending balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.posted_balance_amount
documentation:
Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to lock on the
account’s posted balance. If any of these conditions would be false after the
transaction is created, the entire call will fail with error code 422.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_create_async_params.LedgerTransactionLedgerEntry.show_resulting_ledger_account_balances
documentation:
If true, response will include the balance of the associated ledger account for
the entry.

The highest amount this expected payment may be equal to.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.counterparty_id
documentation: The ID of the counterparty you expect for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.currency
documentation: Must conform to ISO 4217. Defaults to the currency of the internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.date_lower_bound
documentation: The earliest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.date_upper_bound
documentation: The latest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.direction
documentation:
One of credit or debit.

When you are receiving money, use credit. When you are being charged, use debit.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the Internal Account for the expected payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.ledger_transaction_id
documentation: The ID of the ledger transaction linked to the expected payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.reconciliation_filters
documentation: The reconciliation filters you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.reconciliation_groups
documentation: The reconciliation groups you have for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.reconciliation_method
documentation:
One of manual if this expected payment was manually reconciled in the dashboard,
automatic if it was automatically reconciled by Modern Treasury, or null if it
is unreconciled.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.reconciliation_rule_variables
documentation: An array of reconciliation rule variables for this payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.statement_descriptor
documentation:
The statement description you expect to see on the transaction.

For ACH payments, this will be the full line item passed from the bank. For wire
payments, this will be the OBI field on the wire. For check payments, this will
be the memo field.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.status
documentation: One of unreconciled, partially_reconciled, reconciled, or archived.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.transaction_id
documentation: The ID of the Transaction this expected payment object has been matched to.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.transaction_line_item_id
documentation: The ID of the Transaction Line Item this expected payment has been matched to.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.expected_payment.ExpectedPayment.type
documentation:
One of: ach, au_becs, bacs, book, check, eft, interac, provxchange, rtp, sen,
sepa, signet, wire.

When true, the invoice will progress to unpaid automatically and cannot be
edited after entering that state. If the invoice fails to progress to unpaid,
the errors will be returned and the invoice will not be created.

When payment_method is automatic, the fallback payment method to use when an
automatic payment fails. One of `manual` or `ui`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.ingest_ledger_entries
documentation:
Whether to ingest the ledger_entries to populate the invoice line items.

If this is false, then a line item must be provided. If this is true, line_items
must be empty. Ignored if ledger_account_settlement_id is empty.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.invoice_line_items
documentation:
An array of invoice line items.

The API supports a maximum of 50 invoice line items per invoice. If a greater
number of invoice line items is required, please contact support.

documentation: The invoice issuer's business address.

documentation: The ID of the virtual account the invoice should be paid to.

documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.notification_email_addresses
documentation:
Emails in addition to the counterparty email to send invoice status
notifications to. At least one email is required if notifications are enabled

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.notifications_enabled
documentation:
If true, the invoice will send email notifications to the invoice recipients
about invoice status changes.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.payment_effective_date
documentation:
Date transactions are to be posted to the participants' account.

Defaults to the current business day or the next business day if the current day
is a bank holiday or weekend. Format: yyyy-mm-dd.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.payment_method
documentation:
The method by which the invoice can be paid.

    `ui` will show the embedded payment collection flow. `automatic` will
    automatically initiate payment based upon the account details of the
    receiving_account id.
If the invoice amount is positive, the automatically
    initiated payment order's direction will be debit. If the invoice amount is
    negative, the automatically initiated payment order's direction will be credit.
    One of `manual`, `ui`, or `automatic`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.payment_type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

documentation:
The email of the recipient of the invoice.

Leaving this value as null will fallback to using the counterparty's name.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.invoice_create_params.InvoiceCreateParams.recipient_name

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.base_currency
documentation: Currency to convert, often called the "sell" currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.exponent
documentation:
The exponent component of the rate.

The decimal is calculated as `value` / (10 ^ `exponent`).

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.rate_string
documentation: A string representation of the rate.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.target_amount
documentation:
Amount in the lowest denomination of the `target_currency`, often called the
"buy" amount.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.target_currency
documentation: Currency to convert the `base_currency` to, often called the "buy" currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ForeignExchangeRate.value
documentation:
The whole number component of the rate.

The decimal is calculated as `value` / (10 ^ `exponent`).

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ReferenceNumber.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ReferenceNumber.reference_number
documentation: The vendor reference number.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.ReferenceNumber.reference_number_type
documentation: The type of the reference number. Referring to the vendor payment id.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.accounting_category_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.accounting_ledger_class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting ledger classes.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000 (cents). For RTP, the maximum amount
allowed by the network is $100,000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.charge_bearer
documentation:
The party that will pay the fees for the payment order.

Only applies to wire payment orders. Can be one of shared, sender, or receiver,
which correspond respectively with the SWIFT 71A values `SHA`, `OUR`, `BEN`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.compliance_rule_metadata
documentation:
Custom key-value pair for usage in compliance rules.

Please contact support before making changes to this field.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.counterparty_id
documentation:
If the payment order is tied to a specific Counterparty, their id will appear,
otherwise `null`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.currency
documentation: Defaults to the currency of the originating account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.current_return
documentation:
If the payment order's status is `returned`, this will include the return
object's data.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.decision_id
documentation:
The ID of the compliance decision for the payment order, if transaction
monitoring is enabled.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.description
documentation: An optional description for internal use.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.direction
documentation:
One of `credit`, `debit`.

Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. A `credit` moves
money from your account to someone else's. A `debit` pulls money from someone
else's account to your own. Note that wire, rtp, and check payments will always
be `credit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.effective_date
documentation:
Date transactions are to be posted to the participants' account.

Defaults to the current business day or the next business day if the current day
is a bank holiday or weekend. Format: yyyy-mm-dd.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.expires_at
documentation: RFP payments require an expires_at. This value must be past the effective_date.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.foreign_exchange_contract
documentation:
If present, indicates a specific foreign exchange contract number that has been
generated by your financial institution.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.foreign_exchange_indicator
documentation:
Indicates the type of FX transfer to initiate, can be either
`variable_to_fixed`, `fixed_to_variable`, or `null` if the payment order
currency matches the originating account currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.foreign_exchange_rate
documentation: Associated serialized foreign exchange rate information.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.ledger_transaction_id
documentation: The ID of the ledger transaction linked to the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.nsf_protected
documentation:
A boolean to determine if NSF Protection is enabled for this payment order.

Note that this setting must also be turned on in your organization settings
page.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.originating_account_id
documentation: The ID of one of your organization's internal accounts.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.originating_party_name
documentation:
If present, this will replace your default company name on receiver's bank
statement. This field can only be used for ACH payments currently. For ACH, only
the first 16 characters of this string will be used. Any additional characters
will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.priority
documentation:
Either `normal` or `high`.

For ACH and EFT payments, `high` represents a same-day ACH or EFT transfer,
respectively. For check payments, `high` can mean an overnight check rather than
standard mail.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.process_after
documentation:
If present, Modern Treasury will not process the payment until after this time.

If `process_after` is past the cutoff for `effective_date`, `process_after` will
take precedence and `effective_date` will automatically update to reflect the
earliest possible sending date after `process_after`. Format is ISO8601
timestamp.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.purpose
documentation:
For `wire`, this is usually the purpose which is transmitted via the
"InstrForDbtrAgt" field in the ISO20022 file. For `eft`, this field is the 3
digit CPA Code that will be attached to the payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.receiving_account_id
documentation: The receiving account ID. Can be an `external_account` or `internal_account`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.send_remittance_advice
documentation:
Send an email to the counterparty when the payment order is sent to the bank.

If `null`, `send_remittance_advice` on the Counterparty is used.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.statement_descriptor
documentation:
An optional descriptor which will appear in the receiver's statement.

For `check` payments this field will be used as the memo line. For `ach` the
maximum length is 10 characters. Note that for ACH payments, the name on your
bank account will be included automatically by the bank, so you can use the
characters for other useful information. For `eft` the maximum length is 15
characters.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.status
documentation: The current status of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.subtype
documentation:
An additional layer of classification for the type of payment order you are
doing. This field is only used for `ach` payment orders currently. For `ach`
payment orders, the `subtype` represents the SEC code. We currently support
`CCD`, `PPD`, `IAT`, `CTX`, `WEB`, `CIE`, and `TEL`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.transaction_ids
documentation:
The IDs of all the transactions associated to this payment order.

Usually, you will only have a single transaction ID. However, if a payment order
initially results in a Return, but gets redrafted and is later successfully
completed, it can have many transactions.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.transaction_monitoring_enabled
documentation:
A flag that determines whether a payment order should go through transaction
monitoring.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.ultimate_originating_account
documentation:
The account to which the originating of this payment should be attributed to.

Can be a `virtual_account` or `internal_account`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.ultimate_originating_account_id
documentation:
The ultimate originating account ID.

Can be a `virtual_account` or `internal_account`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.ultimate_originating_party_identifier
documentation: Identifier of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.ultimate_originating_party_name
documentation: Name of the ultimate originator of the payment order.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.vendor_attributes
documentation:
Additional vendor specific fields for this payment.

Data must be represented as key-value pairs.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order.PaymentOrder.vendor_failure_reason
documentation:
This field will be populated if a vendor failure occurs.

Logic shouldn't be built on its value as it is free-form.

modules.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalanceAvailableBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalanceAvailableBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalancePendingBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalancePendingBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalancePostedBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalancePostedBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalance.available_balance
documentation:
The available_balance is the sum of all posted inbound entries and pending
outbound entries. For credit normal, available_amount = posted_credits -
pending_debits; for debit normal, available_amount = posted_debits -
pending_credits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalance.pending_balance
documentation: The pending_balance is the sum of all pending and posted entries.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.EndingBalance.posted_balance
documentation: The posted_balance is the sum of all posted entries.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalanceAvailableBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalanceAvailableBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalancePendingBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalancePendingBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalancePostedBalance.currency
documentation: The currency of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalancePostedBalance.currency_exponent
documentation: The currency exponent of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalance.available_balance
documentation:
The available_balance is the sum of all posted inbound entries and pending
outbound entries. For credit normal, available_amount = posted_credits -
pending_debits; for debit normal, available_amount = posted_debits -
pending_credits.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalance.pending_balance
documentation: The pending_balance is the sum of all pending and posted entries.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.StartingBalance.posted_balance
documentation: The posted_balance is the sum of all posted entries.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.description
documentation: The description of the ledger account statement.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.effective_at_lower_bound
documentation:
The inclusive lower bound of the effective_at timestamp of the ledger entries to
be included in the ledger account statement.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.effective_at_upper_bound
documentation:
The exclusive upper bound of the effective_at timestamp of the ledger entries to
be included in the ledger account statement.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.ending_balance
documentation:
The pending, posted, and available balances for this ledger account at the
`effective_at_upper_bound`. The posted balance is the sum of all posted entries
on the account. The pending balance is the sum of all pending and posted entries
on the account. The available balance is the posted incoming entries minus the
sum of the pending and posted outgoing amounts.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.ledger_account_id
documentation:
The id of the ledger account whose ledger entries are queried against, and its
balances are computed as a result.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.ledger_account_lock_version
documentation: Lock version of the ledger account at the time of statement generation.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.ledger_account_normal_balance
documentation: The normal balance of the ledger account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.ledger_id
documentation: The id of the ledger that this ledger account statement belongs to.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.ledger_account_statement_create_response.LedgerAccountStatementCreateResponse.starting_balance
documentation:
The pending, posted, and available balances for this ledger account at the
`effective_at_lower_bound`. The posted balance is the sum of all posted entries
on the account. The pending balance is the sum of all pending and posted entries
on the account. The available balance is the posted incoming entries minus the
sum of the pending and posted outgoing amounts.

modules.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.base_amount
documentation:
Amount in the lowest denomination of the `base_currency` to convert, often
called the "sell" amount.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.base_currency
documentation: Currency to convert, often called the "sell" currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.exponent
documentation:
The exponent component of the rate.

The decimal is calculated as `value` / (10 ^ `exponent`).

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.rate_string
documentation: A string representation of the rate.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.target_amount
documentation:
Amount in the lowest denomination of the `target_currency`, often called the
"buy" amount.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.target_currency
documentation: Currency to convert the `base_currency` to, often called the "buy" currency.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.ForeignExchangeRate.value
documentation:
The whole number component of the rate.

The decimal is calculated as `value` / (10 ^ `exponent`).

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.amount
documentation:
Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.as_of_date
documentation: The date on which the transaction occurred.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.as_of_time
documentation:
The time on which the transaction occurred.

Depending on the granularity of the timestamp information received from the
bank, it may be `null`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.as_of_timezone
documentation:
The timezone in which the `as_of_time` is represented.

Can be `null` if the bank does not provide timezone info.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.currency
documentation: Currency that this transaction is denominated in.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.custom_identifiers
documentation:
An object containing key-value pairs, each with a custom identifier as the key
and a string value.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.direction
documentation: Either `credit` or `debit`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.foreign_exchange_rate
documentation: Associated serialized foreign exchange rate information.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.internal_account_id
documentation: The ID of the relevant Internal Account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.live_mode
documentation:
This field will be true if this object exists in the live environment or false
if it exists in the test environment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.metadata
documentation:
Additional data represented as key-value pairs.

Both the key and value must be strings.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.posted
documentation: This field will be `true` if the transaction has posted to the account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.reconciled
documentation:
This field will be `true` if a transaction is reconciled by the Modern Treasury
system. This means that it has transaction line items that sum up to the
transaction's amount.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.type
documentation:
The type of the transaction.

Examples could be `card, `ach`, `wire`, `check`, `rtp`, `book`, or `sen`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.vendor_code
documentation:
When applicable, the bank-given code that determines the transaction's category.

For most banks this is the BAI2/BTRS transaction code.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.vendor_code_type
documentation:
The type of `vendor_code` being reported.

Can be one of `bai2`, `bankprov`, `bnk_dev`, `cleartouch`, `currencycloud`,
`cross_river`, `dc_bank`, `dwolla`, `evolve`, `goldman_sachs`, `iso20022`,
`jpmc`, `mx`, `signet`, `silvergate`, `swift`, `us_bank`, or others.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.vendor_customer_id
documentation: An identifier given to this transaction by the bank, often `null`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.vendor_id
documentation: An identifier given to this transaction by the bank.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.details
documentation:
This field contains additional information that the bank provided about the
transaction. This is structured data. Some of the data in here might overlap
with what is in the `vendor_description`. For example, the OBI could be a part
of the vendor description, and it would also be included in here. The attributes
that are passed through the details field will vary based on your banking
partner. Currently, the following keys may be in the details object:
`originator_name`, `originator_to_beneficiary_information`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.transaction.Transaction.vendor_description
documentation:
The transaction detail text that often appears in on your bank statement and in
your banking portal.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.accounting_ledger_class_id

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.counterparty_id

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.currency

If `process_after` is past the cutoff for `effective_date`, `process_after` will
take precedence and `effective_date` will automatically update to reflect the
earliest possible sending date after `process_after`. Format is ISO8601
timestamp.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.purpose
documentation:
For `wire`, this is usually the purpose which is transmitted via the
"InstrForDbtrAgt" field in the ISO20022 file. For `eft`, this field is the 3
digit CPA Code that will be attached to the payment.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.receiving_account
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.receiving_account_id
documentation:
Either `receiving_account` or `receiving_account_id` must be present.

When using `receiving_account_id`, you may pass the id of an external account or
an internal account.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.remittance_information
documentation:
For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record.

For `wire` payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to
Beneficiary Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.send_remittance_advice
documentation:
Send an email to the counterparty when the payment order is sent to the bank.

If `null`, `send_remittance_advice` on the Counterparty is used.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.statement_descriptor
documentation:
An optional descriptor which will appear in the receiver's statement.

For `check` payments this field will be used as the memo line. For `ach` the
maximum length is 10 characters. Note that for ACH payments, the name on your
bank account will be included automatically by the bank, so you can use the
characters for other useful information. For `eft` the maximum length is 15
characters.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.status
documentation:
To cancel a payment order, use `cancelled`.

To redraft a returned payment order, use `approved`. To undo approval on a
denied or approved payment order, use `needs_approval`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.subtype
documentation:
An additional layer of classification for the type of payment order you are
doing. This field is only used for `ach` payment orders currently. For `ach`
payment orders, the `subtype` represents the SEC code. We currently support
`CCD`, `PPD`, `IAT`, `CTX`, `WEB`, `CIE`, and `TEL`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.type
documentation:
One of `ach`, `se_bankgirot`, `eft`, `wire`, `check`, `sen`, `book`, `rtp`,
`sepa`, `bacs`, `au_becs`, `interac`, `neft`, `nics`,
`nz_national_clearing_code`, `sic`, `signet`, `provexchange`, `zengin`.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.ultimate_originating_party_identifier
documentation:
This represents the identifier by which the person is known to the receiver when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 22 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.ultimate_originating_party_name
documentation:
This represents the name of the person that the payment is on behalf of when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 15 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.ultimate_receiving_party_identifier
documentation:
This represents the name of the merchant that the payment is being sent to when
using the CIE subtype for ACH payments. Only the first 22 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.PaymentOrderUpdateParams.ultimate_receiving_party_name
documentation:
This represents the identifier by which the merchant is known to the person
initiating an ACH payment with CIE subtype. Only the first 15 characters of this
string will be used. Any additional characters will be truncated.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.Accounting.account_id
documentation:
The ID of one of your accounting categories.

Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.Accounting.class_id
documentation:
The ID of one of the class objects in your accounting system.

Class objects track segments of your business independent of client or project.
Note that these will only be accessible if your accounting system has been
connected.

class_variables.name: modern_treasury.types.payment_order_update_params.LineItem.accounting_category_id

methods.name: modern_treasury._response.APIResponse.parse
documentation:
Returns the rich python representation of this response's data.

For lower-level control, see `.read()`, `.json()`, `.iter_bytes()`.

You can customise the type that the response is parsed into through
the `to` argument, e.g.

```py
from modern_treasury import BaseModel


class MyModel(BaseModel):
    foo: str


obj = response.parse(to=MyModel)
print(obj.foo)
```

We support parsing:
  - `BaseModel`
  - `dict`
  - `list`
  - `Union`
  - `str`
  - `int`
  - `float`
  - `httpx.Response`

been streamed.

This can happen if you use a method like `.iter_lines()` and then attempt
to read th entire response body afterwards, e.g.

```py
response = await client.post(...)
async for line in response.iter_lines():
    ...  # do something with `line`

content = await response.read()
# ^ error
```

If you want this behaviour you'll need to either manually accumulate the response
content or call `await response.read()` before iterating over the stream.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.payment_flows.PaymentFlows.create
documentation:
create payment_flow

Args:
  amount: Required.

Value in specified currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be
      represented as 1000. Can be any integer up to 36 digits.

  counterparty_id: Required. The ID of a counterparty associated with the payment. As part of the
      payment workflow an external account will be associated with this model.

  currency: Required. The currency of the payment.

  direction: Required. Describes the direction money is flowing in the transaction. Can only
      be `debit`. A `debit` pulls money from someone else's account to your own.

  originating_account_id: Required. The ID of one of your organization's internal accounts.

  due_date: Optional. Can only be passed in when `effective_date_selection_enabled` is
      `true`. When set, the due date is shown to your end-user in the pre-built UI as
      they are selecting a payment `effective_date`.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury._base_client.BaseSyncPage.__iter__
documentation: Pydantic uses a custom `__iter__` method to support casting BaseModels
to dictionaries. e.g. dict(model).
As we want to support `for item in page`, this is inherently incompatible
with the default pydantic behaviour. It is not possible to support both
use cases at once. Fortunately, this is not a big deal as all other pydantic
methods should continue to work as expected as there is an alternative method
to cast a model to a dictionary, model.dict(), which is used internally
by pydantic.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.transactions.transactions.Transactions.create
documentation:
create transaction

Args:
  amount: Value in specified currency's smallest unit.

e.g. $10 would be represented
      as 1000.

  as_of_date: The date on which the transaction occurred.

  direction: Either `credit` or `debit`.

  internal_account_id: The ID of the relevant Internal Account.

  vendor_code: When applicable, the bank-given code that determines the transaction's category.
      For most banks this is the BAI2/BTRS transaction code.

  vendor_code_type: The type of `vendor_code` being reported. Can be one of `bai2`, `bankprov`,
      `bnk_dev`, `cleartouch`, `currencycloud`, `cross_river`, `dc_bank`, `dwolla`,
      `evolve`, `goldman_sachs`, `iso20022`, `jpmc`, `mx`, `signet`, `silvergate`,
      `swift`, `us_bank`, or others.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  posted: This field will be `true` if the transaction has posted to the account.

  type: The type of the transaction. Examples could be
      `card, `ach`, `wire`, `check`, `rtp`, `book`, or `sen`.

  vendor_description: The transaction detail text that often appears in on your bank statement and in
      your banking portal.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_transactions.ledger_transactions.LedgerTransactions.create
documentation:
Create a ledger transaction.

Args:
  ledger_entries: An array of ledger entry objects.

  description: An optional description for internal use.

  effective_at: The timestamp (ISO8601 format) at which the ledger transaction happened for
      reporting purposes.

  effective_date: The date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which the ledger transaction happened for reporting
      purposes.

  external_id: A unique string to represent the ledger transaction. Only one pending or posted
      ledger transaction may have this ID in the ledger.

  ledgerable_id: If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
      Treasury, the id will be populated here, otherwise null.

  ledgerable_type: If the ledger transaction can be reconciled to another object in Modern
      Treasury, the type will be populated here, otherwise null. This can be one of
      payment_order, incoming_payment_detail, expected_payment, return, paper_item, or
      reversal.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  status: To post a ledger transaction at creation, use `posted`.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_transactions.ledger_transactions.LedgerTransactions.list
documentation:
Get a list of ledger transactions.

Args:
  id: If you have specific IDs to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query
      parameters delimited with `id[]=`, for example `?id[]=123&id[]=abc`.

  effective_at: Use "gt" (>), "gte" (>=), "lt" (<), "lte" (<=), or "eq" (=) to filter by
      effective at. For example, for all transactions after Jan 1 2000, use
      effective_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T00:00:00:00.000Z.

  effective_date: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by
      effective date. For example, for all dates after Jan 1 2000, use

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.expected_payments.ExpectedPayments.create
documentation:
create expected payment

Args:
  amount_lower_bound: The lowest amount this expected payment may be equal to. Value in specified
      currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

  amount_upper_bound: The highest amount this expected payment may be equal to. Value in specified
      currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

  counterparty_id: The ID of the counterparty you expect for this payment.

  currency: Must conform to ISO 4217. Defaults to the currency of the internal account.

  date_lower_bound: The earliest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

  date_upper_bound: The latest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

  description: An optional description for internal use.

  direction: One of credit or debit. When you are receiving money, use credit. When you are
      being charged, use debit.

  internal_account_id: The ID of the Internal Account for the expected payment.

  ledger_transaction: Specifies a ledger transaction object that will be created with the expected
      payment. If the ledger transaction cannot be created, then the expected payment
      creation will fail. The resulting ledger transaction will mirror the status of
      the expected payment.

  ledger_transaction_id: Either ledger_transaction or ledger_transaction_id can be provided. Only a
      pending ledger transaction can be attached upon expected payment creation. Once
      the expected payment is created, the status of the ledger transaction tracks the
      expected payment automatically.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  reconciliation_filters: The reconciliation filters you have for this payment.

  reconciliation_groups: The reconciliation groups you have for this payment.

  reconciliation_rule_variables: An array of reconciliation rule variables for this payment.

  remittance_information: For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record. For `wire`
      payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to Beneficiary
      Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

  statement_descriptor: The statement description you expect to see on the transaction. For ACH
      payments, this will be the full line item passed from the bank. For wire
      payments, this will be the OBI field on the wire. For check payments, this will
      be the memo field.

  type: One of: ach, au_becs, bacs, book, check, eft, interac, provxchange, rtp, sen,
      sepa, signet, wire.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.expected_payments.AsyncExpectedPayments.create
documentation:
create expected payment

Args:
  amount_lower_bound: The lowest amount this expected payment may be equal to. Value in specified
      currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

  amount_upper_bound: The highest amount this expected payment may be equal to. Value in specified
      currency's smallest unit. e.g. $10 would be represented as 1000.

  counterparty_id: The ID of the counterparty you expect for this payment.

  currency: Must conform to ISO 4217. Defaults to the currency of the internal account.

  date_lower_bound: The earliest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

  date_upper_bound: The latest date the payment may come in. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

  description: An optional description for internal use.

  direction: One of credit or debit. When you are receiving money, use credit. When you are
      being charged, use debit.

  internal_account_id: The ID of the Internal Account for the expected payment.

  ledger_transaction: Specifies a ledger transaction object that will be created with the expected
      payment. If the ledger transaction cannot be created, then the expected payment
      creation will fail. The resulting ledger transaction will mirror the status of
      the expected payment.

  ledger_transaction_id: Either ledger_transaction or ledger_transaction_id can be provided. Only a
      pending ledger transaction can be attached upon expected payment creation. Once
      the expected payment is created, the status of the ledger transaction tracks the
      expected payment automatically.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  reconciliation_filters: The reconciliation filters you have for this payment.

  reconciliation_groups: The reconciliation groups you have for this payment.

  reconciliation_rule_variables: An array of reconciliation rule variables for this payment.

  remittance_information: For `ach`, this field will be passed through on an addenda record. For `wire`
      payments the field will be passed through as the "Originator to Beneficiary
      Information", also known as OBI or Fedwire tag 6000.

  statement_descriptor: The statement description you expect to see on the transaction. For ACH
      payments, this will be the full line item passed from the bank. For wire
      payments, this will be the OBI field on the wire. For check payments, this will
      be the memo field.

  type: One of: ach, au_becs, bacs, book, check, eft, interac, provxchange, rtp, sen,
      sepa, signet, wire.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_account_settlements.LedgerAccountSettlements.create
documentation:
Create a ledger account settlement.

Args:
  contra_ledger_account_id: The id of the contra ledger account that sends to or receives funds from the
      settled ledger account.

  settled_ledger_account_id: The id of the settled ledger account whose ledger entries are queried against,
      and its balance is reduced as a result.

  allow_either_direction: If true, the settlement amount and settlement_entry_direction will bring the
      settlement ledger account's balance closer to zero, even if the balance is
      negative.

  description: The description of the ledger account settlement.

  effective_at_upper_bound: The exclusive upper bound of the effective_at timestamp of the ledger entries to
      be included in the ledger account settlement. The default value is the
      created_at timestamp of the ledger account settlement.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  skip_settlement_ledger_transaction: It is set to `false` by default. It should be set to `true` when migrating
      existing settlements.

  status: The status of the ledger account settlement. It is set to `pending` by default.
      To post a ledger account settlement at creation, use `posted`.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

      "USD".

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_accounts.LedgerAccounts.list
documentation:

Args:
  id: If you have specific IDs to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query
      parameters delimited with `id[]=`, for example `?id[]=123&id[]=abc`.

  available_balance_amount: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to
      filter by balance amount.

  balances: Use `balances[effective_at_lower_bound]` and
      `balances[effective_at_upper_bound]` to get the balances change between the two
      timestamps. The lower bound is inclusive while the upper bound is exclusive of
      the provided timestamps. If no value is supplied the balances will be retrieved
      not including that bound.

  created_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      created at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use
      created_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

  metadata: For example, if you want to query for records with metadata key `Type` and value
      `Loan`, the query would be `metadata%5BType%5D=Loan`. This encodes the query
      parameters.

  name: If you have specific names to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query
      parameters delimited with `name[]=`, for example `?name[]=123&name[]=abc`.

  pending_balance_amount: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to
      filter by balance amount.

  posted_balance_amount: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `eq` (=), or `not_eq` (!=) to
      filter by balance amount.

  updated_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      updated at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use
      updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_entries.LedgerEntries.list
documentation:
Get a list of all ledger entries.

Args:
  id: If you have specific IDs to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query
      parameters delimited with `id[]=`, for example `?id[]=123&id[]=abc`.

  as_of_lock_version: Shows all ledger entries that were present on a ledger account at a particular
      `lock_version`. You must also specify `ledger_account_id`.

  direction: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update
      a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are
      deleted.

  effective_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      transaction's effective time. Format ISO8601

  effective_date: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      transaction's effective date. Format YYYY-MM-DD

  ledger_account_category_id: Get all ledger entries that match the direction specified. One of `credit`,
      `debit`.

  ledger_account_lock_version: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      lock_version of a ledger account. For example, for all entries created at or
      before before lock_version 1000 of a ledger account, use
      `ledger_account_lock_version%5Blte%5D=1000`.

  ledger_account_statement_id: Get all ledger entries that are included in the ledger account statement.

  metadata: For example, if you want to query for records with metadata key `Type` and value
      `Loan`, the query would be `metadata%5BType%5D=Loan`. This encodes the query
      parameters.

  order_by: Order by `created_at` or `effective_at` in `asc` or `desc` order. For example,
      to order by `effective_at asc`, use `order_by%5Beffective_at%5D=asc`. Ordering
      by only one field at a time is supported.

  show_balances: If true, response will include the balances attached to the ledger entry. If
      there is no balance available, null will be returned instead.

  show_deleted: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update
      a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are
      deleted.

  status: Get all ledger entries that match the status specified. One of `pending`,
      `posted`, or `archived`.

  updated_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      posted at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use
      updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.ledger_entries.AsyncLedgerEntries.list
documentation:
Get a list of all ledger entries.

Args:
  id: If you have specific IDs to retrieve in bulk, you can pass them as query
      parameters delimited with `id[]=`, for example `?id[]=123&id[]=abc`.

  as_of_lock_version: Shows all ledger entries that were present on a ledger account at a particular
      `lock_version`. You must also specify `ledger_account_id`.

  direction: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update
      a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are
      deleted.

  effective_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      transaction's effective time. Format ISO8601

  effective_date: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      transaction's effective date. Format YYYY-MM-DD

  ledger_account_category_id: Get all ledger entries that match the direction specified. One of `credit`,
      `debit`.

  ledger_account_lock_version: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      lock_version of a ledger account. For example, for all entries created at or
      before before lock_version 1000 of a ledger account, use
      `ledger_account_lock_version%5Blte%5D=1000`.

  ledger_account_statement_id: Get all ledger entries that are included in the ledger account statement.

  metadata: For example, if you want to query for records with metadata key `Type` and value
      `Loan`, the query would be `metadata%5BType%5D=Loan`. This encodes the query
      parameters.

  order_by: Order by `created_at` or `effective_at` in `asc` or `desc` order. For example,
      to order by `effective_at asc`, use `order_by%5Beffective_at%5D=asc`. Ordering
      by only one field at a time is supported.

  show_balances: If true, response will include the balances attached to the ledger entry. If
      there is no balance available, null will be returned instead.

  show_deleted: If true, response will include ledger entries that were deleted. When you update
      a ledger transaction to specify a new set of entries, the previous entries are
      deleted.

  status: Get all ledger entries that match the status specified. One of `pending`,
      `posted`, or `archived`.

  updated_at: Use `gt` (>), `gte` (>=), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), or `eq` (=) to filter by the
      posted at timestamp. For example, for all times after Jan 1 2000 12:00 UTC, use
      updated_at%5Bgt%5D=2000-01-01T12:00:00Z.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

modules.name: modern_treasury.resources.webhooks
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.webhooks.Webhooks.get_signature
documentation:
To verify that a webhook was actually sent by Modern Treasury, every payload is
signed with a signature that is passed through the `X-Signature` HTTP header.

This method will generate a signature based off of your webhook key which can be
found in the Developer Settings,
https://app.moderntreasury.com/developers/webhooks, and the webhook payload.

You can then compare the generated signature with the signature sent with the
request, if they match then the webhook was sent by Modern Treasury.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.webhooks.Webhooks.validate_signature
documentation: Returns whether or not the webhook payload was sent by Modern Treasury.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.webhooks.AsyncWebhooks.get_signature
documentation:

found in the Developer Settings,

You can then compare the generated signature with the signature sent with the
request, if they match then the webhook was sent by Modern Treasury.

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.virtual_accounts.VirtualAccounts.create
documentation:
create virtual_account

Args:
  internal_account_id: The ID of the internal account that this virtual account is associated with.

  name: The name of the virtual account.

  account_details: An array of account detail objects.

  counterparty_id: The ID of the counterparty that the virtual account belongs to.

  credit_ledger_account_id: The ID of a credit normal ledger account. When money leaves the virtual account,
      this ledger account will be credited. Must be accompanied by a
      debit_ledger_account_id if present.

  debit_ledger_account_id: The ID of a debit normal ledger account. When money enters the virtual account,
      this ledger account will be debited. Must be accompanied by a
      credit_ledger_account_id if present.

  description: An optional description for internal use.

  ledger_account: Specifies a ledger account object that will be created with the virtual account.
      The resulting ledger account is linked to the virtual account for auto-ledgering
      IPDs.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  routing_details: An array of routing detail objects.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request

methods.name: modern_treasury.resources.counterparties.Counterparties.create
documentation:
Create a new counterparty.

Args:
  name: A human friendly name for this counterparty.

  accounts: The accounts for this counterparty.

  email: The counterparty's email.

  ledger_type: An optional type to auto-sync the counterparty to your ledger. Either `customer`
      or `vendor`.

  legal_entity_id: The id of the legal entity.

  metadata: Additional data represented as key-value pairs. Both the key and value must be
      strings.

  send_remittance_advice: Send an email to the counterparty whenever an associated payment order is sent
      to the bank.

  taxpayer_identifier: Either a valid SSN or EIN.

  verification_status: The verification status of the counterparty.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

  idempotency_key: Specify a custom idempotency key for this request
